content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

You are allowed to perform the following operation on it any number of times:


Choose an index iii such that 1<i<N1 \lt i \lt N1<i<N, and any integer XXX (XXX can be negative).
Then, modify the array as follows:

Subtract XXX from Ai‚ąí1A_{i-1}Ai‚ąí1‚Äč (that is, Ai‚ąí1‚ÜíAi‚ąí1‚ąíXA_{i-1} \to A_{i-1} - XAi‚ąí1‚Äč‚ÜíAi‚ąí1‚Äč‚ąíX)
Add 2X2X2X to AiA_iAi‚Äč (that is, Ai‚ÜíAi+2XA_i \to A_i + 2XAi‚Äč‚ÜíAi‚Äč+2X)
Subtract XXX from Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äč (that is, Ai+1‚ÜíAi+1‚ąíXA_{i+1} \to A_{i+1} - XAi+1‚Äč‚ÜíAi+1‚Äč‚ąíX)


Your task is to find the minimum number of operations required to convert the array AAA into a palindrome‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä†.

If it is not possible to do so, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† An integer array AAA of length NNN is called a palindrome iff Ai=AN+1‚ąíiA_i = A_{N+1-i}Ai‚Äč=AN+1‚ąíi‚Äč for every i=1,2,3,‚Ä¶,Ni = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, Ni=1,2,3,‚Ä¶,N.

For example, [‚ąí3,35,35,‚ąí3][-3, 35, 35, -3][‚ąí3,35,35,‚ąí3] is a palindrome, while [12,21][12, 21][12,21] is not.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two line of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN, the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum number of operations required to make the array AAA a palindrome.

If it is not possible to do so, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 instead.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4‚čÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can't convert the array AAA into a palindrome.
Test case 222: We do the following operation:

Choose i=2i = 2i=2, X=‚ąí3X = -3X=‚ąí3. The array AAA becomes [3,0,0,3][3, 0, 0, 3][3,0,0,3].
This is a palindrome.

Test case 333: The array AAA is already a palindrome, so no operations are needed.
